forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,29 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KW260M8QZ8T37A8RZGDDVZ81
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a980
+project: expect
+title: Surface catalog read ops (surface get / surfaces list)
+---
+## What
+Implement the read-only `surface` noun: a catalog describing each of the six adapters (cli/http/browser/gui/file/db) — how each drives, observes, and its in-process mechanism. Source content from `ideas/expect.md` §"Surface adapters" table. This is a static, no-agent, no-system read.
+
+- In `crates/swissarmyhammer-expect`: a `surfaces` module exposing `SurfaceInfo { name: Surface, drive: String, observe: String, mechanism: String, locator_dialect: String, deterministic: bool }` and a `catalog() -> Vec<SurfaceInfo>`.
+- Wire the `surface get <name>` and `surfaces list` ops in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/expect/mod.rs` (replace the stubs) returning `json_result(...)`.
+- Adapters not yet implemented are still *described* here (catalog is documentation of intent); a later surface task flips `available`/`deterministic` truthfully if needed.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `expect surfaces list` returns all six surfaces with drive/observe/mechanism/locator fields populated from the design table.
+- [ ] `expect surface get cli` returns the cli entry; an unknown surface name errors clearly.
+- [ ] cli/http/file/db/browser/gui marked `deterministic: true` (per design — mechanical actuation); the catalog notes agent-fallback is the only non-determinism source.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Unit test: `catalog()` has exactly 6 entries with the expected names.
+- [ ] Tool test: `surface get cli` / `surfaces list` dispatch and return the expected JSON shape.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools surface` passes.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd`.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -52,4 +52,9 @@
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools surface` passes.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd`.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd`.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-26 14:19)
+
+### Blockers
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/surfaces.rs:65` — The surface catalog data—six (Surface, drive, observe, mechanism, locator_dialect) tuples—is duplicated verbatim between the `ROWS` const in `catalog()` (line 65) and the `expected` data in `catalog_matches_the_design_tables()` test (line ~141). If the production catalog is updated, the test data can silently drift out of sync, allowing faulty catalog entries to pass their golden test. Extract the catalog data as a module-level constant `const CATALOG_DATA: &[(Surface, &str, &str, &str, &str)] = &[...];` containing all six tuples, then reuse it in both `catalog()` and the test. This ensures the golden test always validates the production data and prevents drift.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -57,4 +57,4 @@
 ## Review Findings (2026-06-26 14:19)

 ### Blockers
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/surfaces.rs:65` — The surface catalog data—six (Surface, drive, observe, mechanism, locator_dialect) tuples—is duplicated verbatim between the `ROWS` const in `catalog()` (line 65) and the `expected` data in `catalog_matches_the_design_tables()` test (line ~141). If the production catalog is updated, the test data can silently drift out of sync, allowing faulty catalog entries to pass their golden test. Extract the catalog data as a module-level constant `const CATALOG_DATA: &[(Surface, &str, &str, &str, &str)] = &[...];` containing all six tuples, then reuse it in both `catalog()` and the test. This ensures the golden test always validates the production data and prevents drift.
\ No newline at end of file
+- [x] `crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/surfaces.rs:65` — The surface catalog data—six (Surface, drive, observe, mechanism, locator_dialect) tuples—is duplicated verbatim between the `ROWS` const in `catalog()` (line 65) and the `expected` data in `catalog_matches_the_design_tables()` test (line ~141). If the production catalog is updated, the test data can silently drift out of sync, allowing faulty catalog entries to pass their golden test. Extract the catalog data as a module-level constant `const CATALOG_DATA: &[(Surface, &str, &str, &str, &str)] = &[...];` containing all six tuples, then reuse it in both `catalog()` and the test. This ensures the golden test always validates the production data and prevents drift. (accepted: intentional independent golden table; `catalog_matches_the_design_tables()` deliberately re-states the values from ideas/expect.md to fail on drift — sharing a const would make the assertion tautological and destroy the drift-detection value. That vacuous-test anti-pattern was explicitly flagged on a sibling task. No code change.)
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -27,6 +27,17 @@

     Left in `doing` for /review.
   timestamp: 2026-06-26T19:19:06.337605+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kw2pgy87w1m8nweqb0eecyc1
+  text: |-
+    WAIVED review finding (surfaces.rs catalog duplication) — no code change.
+
+    Finding recommended extracting a shared `const CATALOG_DATA` reused by both `catalog()` and the test `catalog_matches_the_design_tables()`. DECLINED.
+
+    Rationale: `catalog_matches_the_design_tables()` is a deliberate GOLDEN test that independently re-states the six adapter rows from ideas/expect.md's surface-adapter table. Its entire purpose is to FAIL if the production catalog drifts from the design. Collapsing both sides onto one shared `CATALOG_DATA` const would make the assertion tautological (const == itself) and destroy all drift-detection value — exactly the vacuous-test anti-pattern that was explicitly flagged on a sibling task. Independent golden tables are a legitimate, intended technique here.
+
+    Action: flipped the finding checkbox to [x] with an accepted-note in the task description. Verified nothing regressed: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-expect -E 'test(surface)'` green and `cargo fmt --check` clean. Task stays in `doing` for /review.
+  timestamp: 2026-06-26T19:29:20.647277+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KW260M8QZ8T37A8RZGDDVZ81
 position_column: doing
